basics.name: Michael Jirasek
basics.label: Research Fellow and Team Leader in Autonomous Chemistry and AI-Driven Laboratory Automation
basics.summary: Interdisciplinary chemist and research leader working at the interface of chemistry, robotics, and machine intelligence. I develop autonomous experimentation platforms and quantitative frameworks for chemical complexity and prebiological selection, with applications to origins-of-life research and closed-loop discovery.

work.name: University of Glasgow
work.summary: Leading a multidisciplinary team at the University of Glasgow developing autonomous chemistry platforms and assembly-theory-based methods for quantifying directed chemical exploration in prebiological systems.
highlights: Leading and managing a team of 9 researchers and graduate students, setting scientific direction and delivery milestones
Developing assembly-theory-derived metrics (including exploration ratio and ensemble assembly) for detecting directed chemical exploration
Designing open-ended peptide polymerization workflows (wet-dry cycling, CDI coupling, enzymatic catalysis) for origins-of-life studies
Integrating LLM-driven planning and literature extraction into automated laboratory workflows (ACRA platform)
Designing and building robotic chemistry hardware and control stacks (CAD, Arduino/ESP32 firmware, Python orchestration)
Building closed-loop systems combining reaction execution, automated sampling, low-field NMR, and iterative decision logic
Secured major research funding, including a $1.25M Alfred P. Sloan Foundation grant

work.name: ETH Zurich
work.summary: Developed peptide-based supramolecular and porous materials, supervised MSc researchers, and contributed to project design and execution.
highlights: Synthesising modified amino acids and peptides using SSPS
Fabricating large supramolecular assemblies and confirming structure with TEM
Designing bio-compatible porous peptide-based metal-organic frameworks
Supervising master students and devising research objectives

work.name: University of Oxford
work.summary: Developed original MATLAB workflows for ring-current analysis from experimental NMR data and combined computational and experimental methods to study aromaticity in macrocycles.
highlights: Conceiving and developing original MATLAB software for predicting ring currents
Parsing NMR data from literature and interpreting using custom software
Computationally (DFT) and experimentally (NMR) studying aromaticity and ring-currents

work.name: Institute of Organic Chemistry and Biochemistry, Czech Academy of Sciences
work.summary: Synthesized and characterized azonia-helicene compounds for bioactive screening and advanced chiroptical/crystallographic characterization workflows.
highlights: Synthesizing library of over 200 novel azonia-helicenes
Full characterization as potential drug candidates for telomerase activity
Screening for racemic conglomerate crystals by detecting second-harmonic generation and XRD
Organic compound synthesis for biological testing

thesis.title: Exploring electronic delocalization in porphyrin oligomers
thesis.summary: Demonstrated experimentally that aromaticity can persist at the nanoscale, including synthesis and study of large porphyrin nanorings and Mobius-topology systems; combined synthetic, spectroscopic, and theoretical analysis of electronic delocalization.

highlights: Synthesis of largest aromatic compound ever reported
Advanced time-resolved infrared techniques
Creating twisted porphyrin rings in Möbius strip shape

thesis.title: Organocatalytic [2+2] photocycloadditions of nitrogen-containing dienes
thesis.summary: Series of substituted bis(allyl)amines were synthesized and characterized. The substrates were tested toward [2+2] cycloadditions promoted by visible light facilitated by flavin organocatalysis.

courses: Advanced Methods and Strategies in Synthesis
highlights: Host-guest chemistry inside helicates
Study of binding cooperativity and allosteric effects
Synthesizing enantiopure carbon-rich alleno-acetylenes

skills.name: Computational and Quantitative Methods
keywords: Integration of simulation and experimental evidence

awards.title: Poster Prize
awards.awarder: 18th International Symposium on Novel Aromatic Compounds (ISNA-18), Sapporo, Japan

awards.title: Poster Prize
awards.awarder: Artemis, Octopus and ULTRA User Meeting, Oxford

awards.title: Best Oral Contribution
awards.awarder: 13th Interdisciplinary Session of Young Biologist, Biochemists and Chemists

certificates.name: Orbitrap Fusion Lumos ThermoFisher Mass Spectrometry Training

teaching.summary: Designing projects for and supervising MSc students (7 in total)
Teaching Physics for 3 colleges (1st year chemistry undergraduate students). Teaching groups of 4–8 students, setting problems and individual assessment
Physical Chemistry laboratories (2nd–3rd year students). Supervised and assessed students (groups of 8–12) during laboratory practicum
